header.headline: IR v66 Test Gates
header.subtitle: A gate ladder is still a decision tree: classify the gate family, choose the correct branch, and only then rerun the full stack.

start.title: What gate class failed?
start.body: Choose the lane that matches the first red test family.

left.title: Fast Structural Gates
left.body: Tokenizer, schema, and compiler-smoke checks failed first.

center.title: Model Behavior Gates
center.body: Probe exactness, renderability, or held-out prompts failed.

right.title: Numerical Safety Gates
right.body: Parity or finite-difference checks are the active blocker.

left_outcome.title: Structure Branch
left_outcome.body: Fix the contract, then re-smoke the family before training.

center_outcome.title: Behavior Branch
center_outcome.body: Use the probe autopsy to identify the failure class, not just the failing prompt.

right_outcome.title: Numerics Branch
right_outcome.body: Do not trust a training result until the numerical path is green again.

finish.title: Promotion Gate
finish.body: Only promote the run after the selected branch and the umbrella gate are both green.

footer.note: Decision trees are useful because they keep operational reasoning deterministic.
